name: Sukiyaki
recipeIngredient: 1/2 cup Chinese snow peas, stems and strings removed
1/4 cup Drained canned sliced bamboo shoots
recipeInstructions:
1. In a small bowl, combine beef broth and dried mushrooms; let stand for 15 minutes.
2. Remove the mushrooms with a slotted spoon, reserving the broth. Trim off and discard mushroom stems, then cut caps into quarters.
3. Add soy sauce and sherry to the broth and mix well. Set aside the broth mixture with mushrooms.
4. Broil steak on a rack in a broiling pan, 5 to 6 inches from the heat source, turning once, until rare, about 3 minutes per side. Remove steak to a cutting board and cut into 1/4-inch thick slices.
5. In a 12-inch nonstick skillet, heat oil over medium-high heat. Add snow peas, scallions, onion, garlic, and ginger; saute for 1 minute.
6. Add the reserved broth mixture and continue cooking for 1 to 2 minutes longer. Move vegetables to one side of the skillet.
7. Add mushrooms, cabbage, and bamboo shoots to the center of the skillet and pour in an additional 1/4 cup broth mixture. Cook until vegetables are tender-crisp, about 3 minutes.
8. Move vegetables to the opposite side of the pan from the snow pea mixture. Add steak slices and remaining broth mixture to the center of the skillet and cook, turning once, until heated through, about 30 seconds per side.
9. Decoratively arrange steak and vegetables on a serving plate and top with any pan juices.
